name: Xodd
author: Luis Bolaños Mures / AGP port
description: Xodd, by Luis Bolaños Mures (2011) — the square-board sibling of Yodd. On an empty square board, players alternate dropping one or two stones of EITHER colour (Black moves first, and only one stone on the opening turn). A group is a set of orthogonally connected like-coloured stones, and at the end of every turn the TOTAL number of groups on the board must be odd. You may pass when it keeps the count odd; two passes in a row end the game, and whoever has FEWER groups of their own colour wins. Because the total is odd, the two counts can never tie — there are no draws. Boards of base 9–13 are recommended.
